description: Founder Nickname in Prose Content — Use nickname 'Kriss' in informal prose contexts on the About page, defined as a codebase constant for future-proofing.

userStories.title: Define Founder Nickname as a Codebase Constant
userStories.description: As a developer adding future content, I want the founder's nickname available as a named constant, so that I never need to guess the correct informal name to use in prose.

acceptanceCriteria.text: src/app/about/page.tsx imports FOUNDER_NICKNAME from src/lib/constants.ts and uses it in the bio string (no hardcoded nickname string in component)
npm run lint -- --fix passes with no errors
npm run type-check passes with no errors

userStories.title: Update Founder Bio to Use Nickname
userStories.description: As a site visitor reading the About page, I want the founder's bio to feel warm and personal, so that I connect with the care home's family-oriented brand.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The bio text in team[0].bio (src/app/about/page.tsx:45) uses 'Kriss' instead of 'Mari Kriss C. Aseniero' in the prose sentence — verified by unit test in tests/unit/app/about/page.test.tsx asserting bio contains 'Kriss' and does not contain 'Mari Kriss C. Aseniero'
The team[0].name field ('Mari Kriss C. Aseniero') remains unchanged — verified by unit test in tests/unit/app/about/page.test.tsx asserting team[0].name === 'Mari Kriss C. Aseniero'
The rendered About page displays 'Kriss founded Casa Colina Care...' in the bio paragraph (browser-verified using dev-browser skill)
npm run lint -- --fix passes with no errors
npm run type-check passes with no errors
npm test -- --run passes with no regressions (includes new unit tests for FOUNDER_NICKNAME export and bio text content)
